framing: Is the machine spending its resources on PRODUCT or on ITSELF? This attributes elapsed TIME (the utilisation denominator, from git commit timestamps) and TOKENS (the cost denominator, from the token log) -- SEPARATELY, because they diverge and the divergence is signal -- to a productive/waste activity taxonomy built entirely from data this repo already keeps. A reporting layer, not new instrumentation.
guardrail: DIAGNOSTIC, NEVER A TARGET (R12 / G5 law). 40% productive-and-correct beats 90% busy-and-wrong. No figure here is a completion gate or a thing to hit -- tuning the machine to make utilisation look good corrupts the very actuals it reports.
generated_from: git log (commit subjects + changed files) + docs/observability/token-log.md + action_needed_register.json

productive_pct.note: time = utilisation denominator (git clock); cost = token denominator (token log). They are tracked separately because they diverge -- the divergence is the signal, not an error.

cost_of_self_maintenance.note: Must be trendable DOWN over windows, or the harness is a treadmill.

value_per_problem.note: productive commits per PROBLEM commit (self-repair + rework + hit-limit). No problems in-window -> ratio null, not infinity.

items.what: D5_account_hierarchy_payments keeps being drawn for BUILD (L2->3) but its stated L3 -- the coupled-world belief-vs-truth GAP -- provably needs work OUTSIDE its 3-file company/billing file_scope: a sim-side payment-behaviour GROUND-TRUTH source, a new company/interfaces/sim_interface payment observable, the ledger consuming it, and a gap measurement. That is a seam-crossing COUPLED TRIAD, not a single in-scope fork. Two questions: (1) SEQUENCING -- build it as ONE expanded-scope D5 atom, or DECOMPOSE into a W-lane sim payment-behaviour source + a seam-observable + the D-lane consumption + an H-lane gap measurement (reusing test_gap_metric)? (2) Is the sim-side payment-behaviour GENERATOR a baseline-fidelity model (buildable, decided blind to P&L) or a CURRICULUM decision reserved to the director under R13? Separately: there is a smaller IN-SCOPE increment available now -- activating the R15 controls the last fork built (wiring reconcile/check_conserved into the ledger's production path, sourcing external control totals from company/billing/invoice.py) -- reaching ~L2.5 without the seam. Worth dispatching that first to keep the product lane moving while the coupled-world L3 is sequenced?
